queue.title: Queue is empty
queue.body: Once encoding is wired, queued and running jobs will appear here.

trash.pageSubhead: Originals removed after a successful encode.
trash.title: Trash is empty
trash.body: Source files moved aside during encoding will appear here, with a configurable retention.

logs.pageSubhead: Per-job ffmpeg output and container logs.
logs.title: No logs yet

home.title: x265-butler
home.body: Open the library to browse scanned files.

routeUnknown.title: Unknown route
routeUnknown.body: There is no '{route}' section. Did you mean '{candidate}'?

localeUnknown.title: Language not supported
localeUnknown.body: This URL uses a language code that is not configured. Falling back to English.

localeMissing.title: Language prefix missing
localeMissing.body: URLs in x265-butler always begin with a language code (e.g. /en/library).

fallback.title: Page not found
fallback.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.

onboardingIncomplete.title: Onboarding not completed
onboardingIncomplete.body: Some core settings are still missing. Finish the onboarding to unlock the library.

staleCache.title: Stale browser cache
staleCache.body: Your browser is rendering an older application bundle than the server expects. A hard refresh usually resolves this: press Ctrl+Shift+R (or Cmd+Shift+R on macOS).

unknown.title: Something went wrong
unknown.body: An unexpected error occurred. Please retry, or share the diagnostic report on the forum.

empty.title: No jobs with logs yet
empty.body: Start an encode from the library to capture per-job ffmpeg output here.

empty.title: Select a job
empty.body: Pick an entry from the list to view its captured ffmpeg log.

note.dockerLogs: Container logs are also available from the host shell via `docker logs x265-butler`.

clear.label: Clear log
clear.warning: Also clears Diagnostics recent-errors, slow-requests and slow-queries (shared log store).

tooltip.attention: Elevated: 20–50% legacy codecs still on disk.
tooltip.critical: Critical: more than 50% of bytes use legacy codecs.

sharesTable.title: Shares Comparison

topFolders.title: Top Folders by Size

topFolders.truncatedTooltip: The aggregation hit the 50 000-row safety cap. Filter by share or reduce depth for a tighter view.

noFiles.headline: No files scanned yet

noFilesForShare.headline: No files in this share
noFilesForShare.body: Pick another share from the toolbar or scan this one to populate it.

containerOverride.hint: Applies to this file only. Global: {global}

containerFallback.title: Container Fallback
containerFallback.description: This file was encoded to MKV because the source format is not fully compatible with MP4.

reason.audio: Audio codec incompatible with MP4 (e.g. TrueHD, DTS)
reason.subtitle: Subtitle codec incompatible with MP4
reason.preflight_unavailable: Format analysis failed — fell back to MKV as safe default

rescan.toastInProgress: A scan is already in progress

button.aria: Export the current library view as a CSV file

empty.tooltip: Nothing to export — library is empty

toast.success: Retry queued. File reset to pending.
toast.notEligible: File cannot be retried in its current state.
toast.error: Could not retry. Try again later.

toast.success: Entry forgotten. The file on disk was not touched.
toast.error: Could not delete the entry. Try again later.
toast.activeJob: This entry is being encoded — stop the job before deleting.
toast.benchRef: This entry is referenced by a benchmark and cannot be deleted.

toast.success: Skipped — file returned to wait state.

skip.tooltip: Skip this file. Encoding stops immediately and the file returns to the library wait state.

toast.success: Skipped — file returned to wait state.

cancel_all.tooltip: Cancel the active encode and clear the queue. All affected files return to the library wait state.

confirm.title: Reset queue?
confirm.message: {count, plural, =0 {No jobs in queue.} one {One file will be reset to the wait state.} other {# files will be reset to the wait state.}}

empty.tooltip: Nothing to reset — queue is empty.

toast.success: Reset complete — {skipped} encoding stopped, {cancelled} removed from queue.
toast.error: Could not reset the queue. Try again.

undo.toastBody: {count, plural, =1 {# job cancelling} other {# jobs cancelling}} — Undo?

active.idleHelper: No jobs in flight. Enqueue from Library.

empty.title: Queue empty
empty.helper: No jobs pending. Encoder is idle or all eligible files are pinned.

handle.label: Drag to reorder {filename}

reorder.announceStart: Picked up {filename}. Use arrow keys to move, Space to drop, Escape to cancel.
reorder.announceCancel: Move cancelled. {filename} returned to its position.

toast.conflict: Queue changed during your edit — refreshed
toast.error: Could not save the new order

empty.headline: No jobs yet
empty.helper: Enqueue a file from Library to start.

error.message: Couldn't load queue status. Try again.

bar.label_count: {count, plural, =0 {Nothing selected} one {# selected} other {# selected}}
bar.max_warning_over: Selection: {count} of max 500 — please reduce by {overflow}

restore.label: Restore ({count})
restore.network_error: Network error — selection preserved, please retry

delete.label: Delete permanently ({count})
delete.network_error: Network error — selection preserved, please retry

restore.confirmHelper: {originalPath} will be moved back. The trash entry will be marked restored.
restore.collisionError: Couldn't restore — a file already exists at the original path. Move or rename it first.
restore.missingError: Trash file missing — investigate file system.

empty.headline: Trash is empty
empty.helper: Files removed by completed encodes appear here for {retentionDays} days.

bell.title: {count, plural, one {# driver warning} other {# driver warnings}}

dri_present_no_driver.title: DRI device present without driver
dri_present_no_driver.detail: vainfo could not enumerate VA-API entrypoints

remediation.title: Check GPU device pass-through
steps: Confirm the host has a working iGPU/dGPU driver
Restart the container and refresh detection

qsv_only_legacy_intel.title: Legacy Intel iGPU detected
qsv_only_legacy_intel.detail: Only the i965 driver is available; AV1 hardware encode is unavailable

remediation.title: Legacy iGPU — informational
remediation.body: Your Intel iGPU uses the legacy i965 path. HEVC encode works; AV1 hardware encode is unavailable. No action required unless you need AV1.

vainfo_binary_missing.title: vainfo binary missing
vainfo_binary_missing.detail: vainfo is not installed in this container image

remediation.title: Re-pull the container image
remediation.body: x265-butler ≥ v2.15.0 ships vainfo + VAAPI drivers by default. Pull the latest image and recreate the container.

nvenc_no_runtime.title: NVIDIA GPU detected without runtime
nvenc_no_runtime.detail: /dev/nvidia* devices present but nvidia-smi is missing

remediation.title: Enable NVIDIA NVENC
steps: Install the unRAID NVIDIA-Driver-Plugin from Community Apps
Add the Variables NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Restart the container and click Test now

encoder_runtime_broken.title: Hardware encoder failed its runtime check
encoder_runtime_broken.detail: A compiled-in encoder could not complete a 1-frame test encode and was removed from the available list

remediation.title: Recover the broken encoder
steps: Re-pull the latest x265-butler image (it ships the matching GPU runtime libraries)
Confirm the GPU driver and runtime are present in the diagnostics report (Container Image section)
Select a different encoder for now (libx265 always works)
File a bug report and include the diagnostics excerpt for the failing encoder

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subhead: At-a-glance overview of the encoding pipeline.

totalSaved.label: Total saved
totalSaved.hint: Total bytes saved across all completed encode jobs — original size minus encoded size.

filesProcessed.label: Files processed
filesProcessed.hint: Number of files successfully encoded in the last 30 days.

avgSavings.label: Avg savings
avgSavings.hint: Average size reduction across completed encodes. 40% means the output file is 40% smaller than the original.

activeEncoder.label: Active encoder
activeEncoder.hint: The hardware or software encoder currently selected. Fallback means the requested encoder was unavailable and a substitute is in use.

queueDepth.label: Queue depth
queueDepth.hint: Total files waiting to be processed — jobs actively encoding plus files pending in queue.

throughput.label: Cumulative throughput
throughput.hint: Average bytes processed per day over the last 30 days. A rough gauge of pipeline velocity.

chart.title: Savings trend
chart.hint: Daily bytes saved over the last 30 days. Each point represents bytes saved by completed encodes on that UTC day. Gaps indicate days with no finished jobs.

empty.title: No encodes yet
empty.body: Encode a file from the Library to start tracking savings.

chart.aria: Savings trend over last 30 days (UTC days). Total saved: {total}. Peak day: {date}, {peak}.

liveQueue.title: Live queue
liveQueue.hint: Real-time view of the currently encoding job. Refreshes automatically every 30 seconds.

recentActivity.title: Recent activity
recentActivity.hint: The 10 most recently finished jobs. Click any row to open the file in the library.

system.title: System
system.hint: Runtime environment — encoder toolchain, CPU count, concurrency limits, and database size.

statsFailed.title: Could not load stats
statsFailed.body: Try again — the server might be busy.

codecDistribution.title: Codec Distribution
codecDistribution.hint: Breakdown of all scanned files by video codec and container format. HEVC share shows how much of the library is already optimised.
codecDistribution.subtitle: {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}} · {bytes}
codecDistribution.hevcCallout: HEVC: {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}} · {percent}% of Library

empty.title: No files indexed yet
empty.body: Run a scan from the Library page to populate codec stats

settings.title: Settings
settings.subhead: Configure how x265-butler discovers and processes your media.

toggle.label: Enable login
toggle.helper: Require username + password to access the dashboard.

disableConfirm.title: Disable login?
disableConfirm.body: The dashboard will become accessible without authentication.

password.label: Password
password.helper: Minimum 12 characters · mix letters, digits, symbols.

error.passwordTooWeak: Password is too weak — needs ≥12 chars and a mix of character classes.
error.usernameInvalid: Username may contain only letters, digits, _ and -.
error.usernameTooShort: Username must be at least 3 characters.

sessionTtl.label: Session lifetime
sessionTtl.helper: How long a logged-in session stays valid before re-auth.

trustProxyXff.label: Trust X-Forwarded-For header
trustProxyXff.helperWarning: Only enable when behind a reverse proxy that strips client-supplied XFF. Otherwise IP rate-limit can be spoofed.

bcryptCost.label: Bcrypt cost factor
bcryptCost.helper: Higher = slower login + stronger against brute force. Default 12 is recommended.
bcryptCost.helperWarning14: Login will take ~1 second per attempt. Operator-only LAN box: this is acceptable; high-traffic deployments would not.

disableAndDelete.label: Disable + delete user
disableAndDelete.body: Removes the user account, clears the session secret, and turns auth off. Reversible only by re-creating the user.
disableAndDelete.confirmBody: Sure? This deletes your user account and turns off authentication.

scanPath.title: Scan path
scanPath.description: Where x265-butler looks for media files.

filter.title: Filter
filter.description: What counts as a candidate for processing.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.description: Language and appearance — applied immediately on save.

encoder.title: Encoder selection
encoder.description: Choose which ffmpeg encoder to use and how many concurrent jobs to run.

crf.title: Per-encoder CRF defaults
crf.description: Quality target per encoder. 0 = lossless, 23 ≈ libx265 default, 51 = worst. Lower numbers mean larger files with better quality.

helper.libx265: Speed-vs-quality tradeoff. Slower presets shrink files further at the same CRF.
helper.nvenc: NVENC preset (p1 = fastest, p7 = slowest+highest quality at same QP).
helper.qsv: QSV preset. Slower = better compression at the same global_quality.
helper.vaapi: VAAPI preset. Driver may ignore this flag — compression_level is the authoritative knob.

applyFromBench.label: Apply from bench
applyFromBench.tooltipNoBench: Run a benchmark first to enable this action.
applyFromBench.tooltipAuthRequired: Sign in to access bench recommendation.
applyFromBench.tooltipFetchError: Could not load bench recommendation — try again later.
applyFromBench.tooltipNoRecommendationsForMode: No recommendations available for this run × mode.

aria.label: Apply CRF values from latest completed bench recommendation

error.toast: Could not apply bench recommendation — try again.

minSavings.title: Minimum savings threshold
minSavings.description: Encoded outputs are only kept when smaller than the source by at least this percentage. Below the threshold the source is preserved and a forensic record is written.

outputContainer.title: Output container
outputContainer.description: Choose the container format for encoded files. MKV preserves all subtitle and audio tracks; MP4 broadens device compatibility but may drop incompatible tracks.

sidecar.title: Sidecar metadata
sidecar.description: Where to write the per-file .x265-butler.json sidecar that lets future scans skip files already processed.

outputMode.title: Output strategy
outputMode.description: Whether the encoded file is written as a new sibling next to the original, or replaces the original in place.

scanRoot.label: Scan path
scanRoot.helper: Absolute path to your media share, e.g. /media.
scanRoot.warningMissing: Path does not currently exist on disk.

extensions.label: File extensions
extensions.helper: File extensions to scan, comma-separated. Example: mp4, mkv.

minSizeMb.label: Minimum file size (MB)
minSizeMb.helper: Files smaller than this are ignored. 50 MB filters samples and thumbnails.

maxDepth.label: Maximum scan depth
maxDepth.helper: How deep into subdirectories to scan. Default 12 covers most libraries.

autoEnqueueAfterScan.label: Auto-encode pending after scan
autoEnqueueAfterScan.helper: When enabled, every scan complete triggers libx265 encode jobs for all files in pending status.

deleteOriginalAfterEncode.label: Delete original after successful encode
deleteOriginalAfterEncode.helper: When enabled, the original file is hard-deleted after a successful encode (skips trash). Default: original moves to trash with retention. Cannot be undone.

helper.mkv: Free-form label appended to the base filename. If the value does not already end in .mkv, .mkv is appended automatically. Example: -x265 → movie-x265.mkv. No path separators or control characters.
helper.mp4: Free-form label appended to the base filename. If the value does not already end in .mp4, .mp4 is appended automatically. Example: -x265 → movie-x265.mp4. No path separators or control characters.
helper.matchSource: Free-form label appended to the base filename. The trailing extension follows the source file's extension at encode time (.mp4 source → .mp4 output; everything else → .mkv). Example: -x265 → movie-x265.mkv (or movie-x265.mp4 if source was .mp4). No path separators or control characters.

outputContainer.label: Output container
outputContainer.description: MP4 trades subtitle and exotic-audio coverage for broader device playback. MKV is the safe default and preserves every track. Match source picks per source extension and falls back to MKV on incompatible audio or subtitles.

warning.mp4: MP4 cannot mux every subtitle and audio codec. Incompatible subtitle streams are dropped automatically and exotic audio codecs (TrueHD, DTS, FLAC, raw PCM) cause the affected job to fail with an actionable error.

advisory.queueSemantic: This setting applies to jobs dispatched after save — including jobs already in the queue.
advisory.matchSource: Picks MKV or MP4 based on the source file extension. Auto-falls back to MKV if the MP4 muxer cannot handle the audio or subtitle codecs — the encoded filename reflects the resolved container.

cachePoolPath.label: Cache pool path
cachePoolPath.helper: Working directory for staged encodes. Should live on fast storage and must NOT overlap the scan root.
cachePoolPath.hintWillCreate: Path does not exist yet — will be created automatically on first encode.

encoder.label: Encoder
encoder.helper: auto resolves to the best detected encoder per job. Override to pin a specific encoder.

concurrency.label: Concurrency
concurrency.helper: auto: cores/4 for libx265 (max 8); 1 for HW encoders. Override applies to ALL encoders uniformly.

crf_libx265.label: CRF — libx265
crf_libx265.helper: 0 = lossless, 23 = default, 51 = worst. Lower means larger file with better quality.

crf_nvenc.label: CRF — NVENC
crf_nvenc.helper: NVENC QP scale 0-51. 23 ≈ libx265 default quality.

crf_vaapi.label: CRF — VAAPI
crf_vaapi.helper: VAAPI QP scale 0-51. 22 = community-default for HEVC.

helper.template: Output kept only when ≥ {value}% smaller than source. Below threshold → status 'Not worth re-encoding'.

sidecarMode.label: Sidecar location
sidecarMode.description: Off writes no sidecar. Beside writes it next to each file (default). Central writes one mirrored tree under the path below, keeping your media folders clean.

sidecarCentralPath.label: Central sidecar path
sidecarCentralPath.description: Absolute path for the central sidecar tree. Used only when the location is set to Central.

outputMode.label: Output strategy

options.suffix: Keep original, write a new file (default)

outputMode.description: Keep original writes the encoded file as a sibling using your output suffix. Replace moves the original to the recoverable trash and renames the encoded file into its place.
outputMode.replaceWarning: Replace moves each original to the trash and puts the encoded file in its place — there is no second copy left on disk. Originals stay recoverable from the trash until retention expires. Hardlinked files (e.g. Sonarr/Radarr imports) automatically fall back to keep-original to avoid breaking the other link.
outputMode.armedConfirmed: In-place replace enabled — save to apply.
outputMode.offReplaceHint: Sidecar metadata is off while replace is on. With no sidecar, skip-detection relies on the database hash alone — a re-scan after a database reset may re-encode an already-HEVC file.
outputMode.armRequired: Confirm the in-place replace warning before saving.

active.fallbackLine: Falling back to libx265 — chosen encoder ({requested}) not detected on this host

unsavedChanges.title: Discard changes?
unsavedChanges.body: Your unsaved changes will be lost.

error.validation: Some fields have errors. Fix them before saving.
error.network: Couldn't save settings. Check your connection and try again.

fieldErrors.cachePoolPathNotWritable: Path is not writable. Check that the directory exists and the container user has write permission.

validation.extensionsRequired: At least one extension is required.
validation.cachePathCollidesWithScanRoot: Cache pool path must not equal the scan root.
validation.outputSuffixFormat: Must not contain '/', '\\' or control characters
validation.sidecarCentralPathAbsolute: Must be an absolute path starting with /
validation.sidecarCentralPathForbidden: That path is a protected system directory — choose another location

shares.title: Shares
shares.emptyState: No shares yet — add your first one below
shares.summary: {name} · {path} · min {minMb, number} MB · {extCount, plural, one {# ext} other {# ext}} · depth {depth}

delete.success: Share deleted — {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}} marked as orphaned

warning.rescanRecommended: Path updated — files at old path will appear as vanished on next scan unless they exist at new path. Re-scan recommended.

discardDialog.title: Discard unsaved changes to {name}?
discardDialog.body: You have unsaved edits on this share. Switching cards will lose them.

onboarding.alreadyCompleted: Onboarding already completed — manage shares in Settings → Paths

error.share_mutating_during_scan: Cannot modify share while a scan is in progress. Wait for scan to finish, then retry.
error.path_nul_byte_rejected: Path may not contain control characters
error.extensions_csv_empty_after_normalization: Extensions list is empty after normalization
error.name_invalid_chars: Name may only contain letters, numbers, spaces, and ._-()
error.cache_pool_path_nested_under_share: Cache pool path collides with share '{conflictingShareName}'
error.share_path_nested: Path conflicts with existing share '{conflictingShareName}' ({conflictingSharePath}) — shares cannot nest

autoScan.title: Auto-Scan
autoScan.description: Watches share paths in real time and enqueues new files within seconds.
autoScan.toggleHelp: Default ON. Off pauses event-driven scanning; the 6 h reconcile timer also stops.
autoScan.inotifyBudgetBannerLow: inotify max_user_watches is below the recommended 524288. Watcher may fail under load.
autoScan.inotifyBudgetBannerPressure: inotify watch budget over 80% — consider raising the limit.
autoScan.enospcWarningBody: The container cannot register more file watches. Raise fs.inotify.max_user_watches.
autoScan.enospcAutoRetryHint: Auto-retry running (backoff 10 s → 60 s → 5 min → 30 min → 1 h).
autoScan.advancedSectionDescription: Operator-tunable detection timing. Defaults are safe; lower stability/batch values increase responsiveness at the cost of more partial-file events.
autoScan.bootScanOnStartHelp: When enabled (default), the container performs a full library reconcile every time it starts. When disabled, files added while the container was stopped are picked up only at the next periodic reconcile (default 6 h).
autoScan.stabilityThresholdHelp: How long chokidar waits after the last write before considering a file stable.
autoScan.batchWindowHelp: Events within this window are coalesced into a single ingest pass per share.
autoScan.reconcileIntervalHelp: Periodic full-library reconcile against the database. Catches anything missed by the event stream.
autoScan.advancedSaveFailedToast: Could not save advanced settings — please retry.

cachePath.title: Cache pool path
cachePath.description: Working directory for in-progress encodes. Auto-detected when left unset.
cachePath.helperText: Leave empty to auto-detect (/mnt/cache → /config/cache). Set an absolute path to override.
cachePath.advisory: No dedicated cache pool detected — encodes will stage inside the appdata volume. For large libraries, map a dedicated cache mount: a full appdata volume risks database corruption.

errorToast.generic: Could not save the cache path
errorToast.cache_pool_path_not_writable: That path is not writable — create it first or pick another
errorToast.cache_pool_path_nested_under_share: That path is nested under a configured share — pick a path outside your media shares

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: System state, warnings, and bug-report surface

copyReport.description: Copy a markdown-formatted diagnostics report to the clipboard for pasting into a bug report.
copyReport.fallbackInstruction: Clipboard access unavailable — select all (Ctrl+A) and copy (Ctrl+C):
copyReport.gateHelper: Run a test encode first to attach lifecycle evidence to the bug report.
copyReport.gateAriaDescription: Copy disabled until test encode completes.

testEncode.killedTimeout: Spawn killed at timeout — check ffmpeg or rate-limit

hint.qsvMfxSessionUnsupported: QSV could not open an MFX session (-9). Usually the container is missing the render group or the oneVPL runtime. Check the GPU / render-group diagnostics.
hint.qsvMfxGenUnsupported: QSV reported an unsupported configuration (-3). This iGPU generation may not support HEVC QSV — consider the libx265 software encoder.
hint.qsvRuntimeMissing: The oneVPL / MFX runtime could not be loaded. This image is missing libmfx-gen / libvpl — update to a build that includes the oneVPL runtime.
hint.qsvMfxSessionGeneric: QSV failed to initialise an MFX session. Verify the iGPU is passed through and the oneVPL runtime plus render group are present.
hint.qsvDeviceCreationFailed: QSV could not create the hardware device. Check that /dev/dri is mapped into the container and the render group matches.
hint.qsvOptionRejected: The QSV encoder rejected an ffmpeg option (oneVPL/libvpl reported an invalid argument). This is usually an incompatible or legacy encoder option, NOT a hardware fault — update to a build with compatible QSV options or report the exact option shown above.
hint.nvencLibMissing: NVENC libraries could not be loaded. Run the container with --runtime=nvidia and NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES including 'video'.
hint.nvencNoCapableDevice: No NVENC-capable GPU was found. Ensure NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES exposes the GPU and the driver supports HEVC NVENC.
hint.nvencSessionFailed: NVENC could not open an encode session. This often means the driver lacks the 'video' capability or the session limit was reached.
hint.nvencDriverMismatch: The NVIDIA driver is too old for this NVENC build. Update the host NVIDIA driver.
hint.vaapiNoDisplay: VAAPI could not open a display device. Check that /dev/dri/renderD128 is mapped and a VA driver (iHD) is installed.
hint.vaapiDeviceMissing: The render device /dev/dri/renderD128 was not found in the container. Map /dev/dri into the container.
hint.permRenderNodeEacces: Permission denied on the render device. The container user is not in the render group — add the render group GID.
hint.genericHwInitFailed: The hardware encoder failed to start. Check the render group, GPU pass-through and the GPU diagnostics section.

feedback.toastBugCopied: Diagnostics-report copied — paste into forum post
feedback.toastFeatureCopied: Feature-request template copied — paste into forum post
feedback.bugGateHelper: Run a test encode first to attach lifecycle evidence to the bug report.

restorePill.label: {count, plural, one {# warning hidden} other {# warnings hidden}}

announce.restored: {count, plural, one {# warning restored} other {# warnings restored}}

blocklist.title: Blocklist Evaluation
blocklist.pathDisclosureWarning: Paths shown here are included verbatim in the bug-report Markdown. Review before posting to public forums.

containerImage.title: Container Image
containerImage.refreshRateLimited: Refresh limited — try again in a moment.

renderDevices.title: Render Devices
renderDevices.groupNote: Group name is container-side and informational; the numeric GID is authoritative. Membership counts the process primary GID (PGID) and supplementary groups — fix via PGID='<gid>' or --group-add '<gid>'.

cpu.title: CPU / iGPU
cpu.gentableNote: HEVC-QSV reflects iGPU hardware capability derived from the embedded CPUID gen-table; actual QSV functionality is verified separately by the probe-encode.

cache.title: Cache
cache.advisory: Config-fallback: no dedicated cache mount detected — map one (a full appdata volume risks database corruption).

step1.headline: Welcome to x265-butler
step1.body: Let's get you set up in four quick steps.

step3.headline: Where are your videos?
step3.body: x265-butler will scan this directory for HEVC-eligible files.

scanRoot.label: Scan root
scanRoot.helper: unRAID typical: /media (read) or /mnt/user/Movies. Must be reachable inside the container.

minSizeMb.label: Minimum file size (MB)
minSizeMb.helper: Files smaller than this are skipped. Filters samples and thumbnails.

extensions.label: Extensions
extensions.helper: Default: mp4, mkv, avi, mov, m4v, ts. Edit later in Settings.

writableGate.warningHeading: This path is read-only inside the container
writableGate.warningBody: x265-butler can read this directory but cannot write to it. Encodes write the optimized file back in-place, so the job will fail late with an EACCES error at the final commit step.
writableGate.fixHint: Fix: set PUID/PGID so the container user owns (or can write) this mount, then restart the container. You can continue now and fix it afterward.

error.fallback: Detection failed. Continuing with libx265 software fallback.

cpuAdvisory.label: Your Intel iGPU (graphics gen {gen}) predates hardware HEVC-QSV (introduced at gen {minGen}, Skylake). QSV is unavailable on this hardware — libx265 software encoding is recommended.

step5.headline: Quality defaults
step5.body: These work for most content. Tune later in Settings → Encoder.

intent.body: Detected GPU support before we set up your library.

nvidia.title: NVIDIA plugin missing
nvidia.body: /dev/nvidia* devices present but nvidia-smi is missing. Install the unRAID NVIDIA-Driver-Plugin to enable NVENC.

steps.1: Install the unRAID NVIDIA-Driver-Plugin from Community Apps
steps.3: Add the Variables NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
steps.4: Restart the container and click Test now

software.title: Software encoding active
software.body: No GPU detected. x265-butler will use libx265 (CPU encoder).

nvidiaHint.title: Running an NVIDIA GPU?
nvidiaHint.body: If you have an NVIDIA card, NVENC needs three things in the container template — copy each into its unRAID field:

legacyIntel.title: Legacy Intel iGPU detected
legacyIntel.body: Your Intel iGPU uses the legacy i965 path. HEVC hardware encode works, but AV1 hardware encode is unavailable and HEVC quality may be inferior to modern iHD iGPUs.

autoScan.heading: Auto-scan runs in the background
autoScan.bodyAutoScanOn: Auto-scan is ON — no manual scans needed for daily use.
autoScan.bodyBootScan: Boot-scan is ON — restarts reconcile anything added during downtime.
autoScan.bodyAdvancedOptions: Power-tuning is available under Settings → General → Advanced Options.

validation.scanRootRequired: Path is required and must be absolute (start with /)

addPattern.headline: Add pattern
addPattern.label: Path pattern
addPattern.helper: Trailing `*` matches anything after; leading + trailing `*` matches contained substring.

extensionWarning.title: Pattern saved — but no scanned share covers .{ext} files
extensionWarning.body: Current scan-extensions: {exts}. Add .{ext} to a share's extensions in Settings to make this pattern effective.

tooltip.neverMatched: Never matched in the last 50 scan evaluations. Current scan-extensions: {exts}. Pattern targets .{ext} — add to a share's extensions in Settings.

empty.headline: No blocklist entries
empty.body: Add files or path patterns to skip them on next scan.

error.patternTooComplex: Pattern can have at most two `*` wildcards.
error.patternTooShort: Pattern must be at least 2 characters.
error.dbError: Could not load blocklist. See server logs.

error.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Try again in {seconds} seconds.

recovery.note: Forgot password? Run scripts/auth-reset.ts from the container shell.

setupRequired.title: Setup required
setupRequired.body: Authentication is enabled but no account exists yet. Open Settings → Auth to create one.

stats.subtitle: Detailed encoding metrics and library analysis.

timeline.subtitle: Daily savings and cumulative total over the last 30 days.

empty.title: No activity yet
empty.body: Savings trend will appear after encoding jobs complete.

pipelineHealth.title: Pipeline Health
pipelineHealth.subtitle: Job queue health and failure metrics

failedJobRate.title: Failed Job Rate
failedJobRate.subtitle: Failure rate across all jobs
failedJobRate.hint: Share of jobs that failed. Below 5% is normal; above 10% signals a recurring problem worth investigating.

encodeSpeedRatio.title: Encode Speed Ratio
encodeSpeedRatio.subtitle: Avg output/input size ratio
encodeSpeedRatio.hint: Ratio of encode duration to video duration. 2× means a 2-hour film encodes in 1 hour. High values may indicate slow hardware or heavy encoder settings.

allTimeJobSummary.title: All-Time Jobs
allTimeJobSummary.hint: Total jobs processed since launch — a quick gauge of how much of your library has been encoded.

fileStatusDistribution.title: File Status
fileStatusDistribution.subtitle: Distribution by current status
fileStatusDistribution.hint: Current processing status of every file the scanner has seen.

empty.title: No files
empty.body: No files have been scanned yet.

resolutionDistribution.title: Resolution Breakdown
resolutionDistribution.hint: How your library files break down by resolution. Useful for targeting the highest-quality files first.

empty.title: No resolution data
empty.body: Resolution data appears after files are scanned.

bitrateDistribution.title: Bitrate Distribution
bitrateDistribution.hint: Distribution by original bitrate. High-bitrate files (> 10 Mbps) typically yield the largest space savings after re-encoding.

skipTypeBreakdown.title: Skip Reasons
skipTypeBreakdown.subtitle: Why files were skipped
skipTypeBreakdown.hint: Why files were skipped by the scanner. A large 'Already HEVC' share means most of your library is already optimised.

currentTrashSize.title: Trash Size
currentTrashSize.subtitle: Currently in trash
currentTrashSize.hint: Disk space held by original files awaiting trash expiry. Released automatically when the retention period ends.

expiringSoon.title: Expiring Soon
expiringSoon.hint: Original files in the trash expiring within the next 7 days — they will be permanently deleted.

caveat.tooltip: Accounts for trash retention. May be negative if files were restored or trash not yet purged.

avgQueueWait.title: Avg Queue Wait
avgQueueWait.subtitle: Average wait before encoding starts
avgQueueWait.hint: Average time a file waits in the queue before encoding starts. Long waits may indicate the encoder is busy or under-provisioned.

topSavers.hint: Top 10 files by absolute bytes saved. Click a filename to jump to its library entry.

savingsTimeline.subtitle: Last 30 days, daily
savingsTimeline.hint: Daily bytes saved and cumulative total over the last 30 days (UTC buckets). Gaps or flat periods indicate no completed encodes on those days.

day.aria: {date}: {savings} saved ({jobCount, plural, =0 {no jobs} one {# job} other {# jobs}})

empty.title: No activity yet
empty.body: Savings trend will appear after encoding jobs complete.

encoderPerf.hint: Total bytes saved per encoder across all completed jobs. Longer bar = more storage reclaimed by that encoder.

empty.title: No encoder data
empty.body: Encoder stats appear after encoding jobs complete.

page.title: Encoder Benchmark
page.subhead: Measure VMAF / size / encode-time for your encoder × preset × quality matrix on representative samples.

what.heading: What it does
what.body: The bench encodes short samples from your selected files across every combination of encoder, preset, and quality value. It measures VMAF (perceptual quality), output size, and encode time — then draws the Pareto frontier so you can spot the best trade-offs at a glance.

modes.heading: Modes
modes.native: Native Sweep — you pick raw quality values (CRF for libx265, CQ for NVENC/QSV, −qp for VAAPI). Cheapest: each combo encodes once.
modes.vmaf: VMAF-anchored — you pick target VMAF scores (e.g. 95, 92, 88). The bench probes each encoder/preset pair to find the quality value that hits each target. ~3–4× more encodes than Native Sweep.

pareto.heading: Pareto frontier
pareto.body: A combo is on the Pareto frontier when no other combo beats it on both size and quality simultaneously. The frontier (grey line in the chart) is the set of non-dominated trade-offs — every point on it is optimal for some priority.

top3.heading: Top 3 picks
top3.size: Smallest — lowest output size on the frontier.
top3.balanced: Balanced — best size×quality product (geometric midpoint on the frontier).
top3.quality: Highest quality — best VMAF score on the frontier.

samples.heading: Samples
samples.body: To keep run times short, the bench only encodes 3×20-second clips per file (configurable in Settings → Bench). Results are representative but not identical to a full-file encode.

passes.heading: Two-pass structure
passes.body: Pass 1 (this plan) encodes the sample matrix and surfaces the Pareto results. Pass 2 — full-file verification of the winner — lands in Plan 11-03.

emptyState.title: No benchmarks yet
emptyState.body: Pick a few files from your library and choose an encoder × preset × quality matrix. The bench encodes 3×20-second samples per file and shows you the Pareto frontier.

mode.label: Mode
mode.native: Native Sweep (raw CRF / CQ values, ~1× cost)
mode.help: Native sweep is the cheap honest baseline. VMAF-anchored solves for cross-encoder fair comparison.
mode.hint: Native Sweep encodes once per combo at a raw quality value — fast and cheap. VMAF-anchored probes each encoder/preset pair to find the quality value that hits your target VMAF score (~3–4× more encodes, but enables fair cross-encoder comparison).

files.label: File IDs
files.hint: Comma-separated IDs from the Library page. The bench encodes short samples, so pick files representative of your actual content — diverse resolution, bitrate, and scene complexity.

encoders.label: Encoders
encoders.hint: libx265 — software CPU encoder, universally available. NVENC — NVIDIA GPU (much faster). QSV — Intel Quick Sync GPU. VAAPI — Linux GPU encoder (AMD or Intel). Only detected/installed encoders will produce results.

presets.label: Presets
presets.hint: Select which x265 presets to benchmark. Faster presets trade quality for speed.

nativeValues.label: Native quality values
nativeValues.help: CRF for libx265 / CQ for NVENC / global_quality for QSV / -qp for VAAPI.
nativeValues.hint: Raw quality parameter fed directly to the encoder. CRF for libx265 — lower = better quality + larger file. CQ for NVENC/QSV. −qp for VAAPI. Typical range 18–30. Each value produces one encode per encoder/preset combo.

vmafTargets.label: VMAF targets
vmafTargets.help: Each encoder is probed to hit each target.
vmafTargets.hint: The bench binary-searches the quality parameter to produce exactly this VMAF score per encoder/preset pair. Higher = better quality + larger file. Typical: 95 (high), 92 (balanced), 88 (small). ~3–4× more encodes than Native Sweep.

summary.native: Native · {encCount, plural, one {# encoder} other {# encoders}} · {presetCount, plural, one {# preset} other {# presets}} · CRF {values}
summary.vmaf: VMAF · {encCount, plural, one {# encoder} other {# encoders}} · {presetCount, plural, one {# preset} other {# presets}} · VMAF {values}

errors.submitFailed: Could not enqueue benchmark — see toast for details

chart.title: Pareto frontier
chart.subhead: Bubble size = encode-time. X is log-scale.

top3.projectionAssumption: Projection assumes uniform compressibility across files
top3.verifyTooltipReady: Run a bench to enable Pass-2 verify
top3.noCandidate: No candidate — Pareto front too short

pass2.heading: Pass-2 verified result
pass2.verifyTooltipReady: Run a bench to enable Pass-2 verify

apply.dialogBody: These values will be written as your global encoder defaults. Pipeline V2 reads them at encode-dispatch.
apply.errorValidation: Cannot apply: combo is not verified
apply.errorNetwork: Could not write defaults — check connection and retry
apply.errorUnauthorized: Sign in required to apply defaults

undo.error: Restore failed — please revert manually in Settings

toast.error: Could not cancel — please try again

sampleCount.label: Samples per file
sampleCount.help: 3 is the sweet-spot per ab-av1 research.

sampleDurationSeconds.label: Sample duration (s)
sampleDurationSeconds.help: 20 seconds per sample is the published sweet-spot.

mode.label: Default Mode
mode.help: Initial mode in the enqueue form. Operator can override per run.

encoders.label: Default Encoders
encoders.help: Pre-selected encoders. At least one required.

nativeValues.label: Default Native Values
nativeValues.help: CRF or QP values to sweep, comma-separated. Example: 23,28

vmafModel.label: VMAF model
vmafModel.help: Standard libvmaf 2.x model. Use vmaf_v0.6.1_neg to penalize NVENC pre-filtering.

vmafBuckets.label: VMAF Pareto Buckets
vmafBuckets.help: Three VMAF thresholds for Pareto bucketing. Example: 95,92,88
vmafBuckets.legacyFormatRecovered: Stored VMAF buckets were invalid — defaults (95/92/88) loaded. Please review or adjust.

errors.vmafBuckets: VMAF buckets must be exactly 3 strictly descending values 0–100 (e.g. 95,92,88).
